nameMeanings.name: Edwina
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 19th-century coinage, representing a Latinate feminine form of Edwin or, in at least one case, of Edward . Edwina Ashley ( 1901–60 ), a descendant of Lord Shaftesbury who became the wife of Earl Mountbatten , was so named in honour of Edward VII ; the king had originally wished her to be called Edwardina.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Edwina was born about 1420, in Yorkshire, England. She had at least 5 sons and 8 daughters with Sir Ralph Pudsay. She was buried in Stanwick Saint John, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Edwinaは約1420年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた。彼女はSir Ralph Pudsayとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と8人の娘をもうけた。彼女はイギリスヨークシュアイアスタンウィック サイント ジョンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edwina(은)는 대략 1420년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir Ralph Pudsay(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 요르크실르 스탠윅 세인트 존에 묻혔다.
